title: Create a Django project with a custom CSS file

source.module: Core Django Fundamentals ‚Äì Templates & Static Content

topicTags: Core Django Fundamentals ‚Äì Templates & Static Content

content.problemStatement:
In this worked-out example, we'll create a simple Django project and add a custom CSS file and image to style a basic homepage. 

### How does this code work?
1. If you review the folder structure - you will see the following
```python
mysite/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ manage.py
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ mysite/              # Project folder
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ settings.py      # STATIC_URL, STATICFILES_DIRS go here
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ...
‚îÇ
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ static/              # Project-level static folder
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ css/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ style.css
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ images/
‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ django.png
‚îÇ
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ home/                # Django app for the webpage
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ views.py
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ...
```
2. The code snippet below is from our project's `settings.py` file and configures how static files (like CSS, JavaScript, and images) are handled.\
`STATIC_URL = 'static/'` - This defines the URL prefix for static files in your project.
```python
STATIC_URL = 'static/'
STATICFILES_DIRS = [
    BASE_DIR / "static",
]
```
3. In `home.html` - you will find the following key code elements with respect to static files
   - `{% load static %}` loads the static template engine, allowing you to use `{% static %}` in your HTML.
   - `` tells Django to include a CSS file located at `static/css/style.css` \
   - `` inserts an image located at `static/images/django.png`

Run the code to see how the static files are served and appear on the homepage.
